eventMetadata.context_description: India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecasts a maximum temperature of 37°C in Lucknow on May 1 under partly cloudy skies with spells of rain or thundershowers, driving trader consensus toward moderate outcomes while favoring cooler readings. The 48% implied probability for 34°C or below reflects post-heatwave relief, as severe conditions peaking at 43.2°C on April 27 have eased with thunderstorms and 3-5°C drops from increased convective activity and cloud cover since April 28. Pre-monsoon moisture from western disturbances suppresses peaks below May climatological averages near 41°C, though model uncertainty persists amid evolving forecast guidance. Watch IMD's daily bulletins for refined ensemble projections ahead of resolution.

markets.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be between $74,000 and $76,000 on April 29?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

markets.question: Will Sheffield Wednesday FC vs. West Bromwich Albion FC end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 2, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.
markets.groupItemTitle: Draw (Sheffield Wednesday FC vs. West Bromwich Albion FC)

events.title: Sheffield Wednesday FC vs. West Bromwich Albion FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming EFL Championship game, scheduled for Saturday, May 2, 2026 between Sheffield Wednesday FC and West Bromwich Albion FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: West Bromwich Albion's 63% implied probability reflects trader consensus on their superior Championship standing (21st with 51 points after a recent two-point deduction) and unbeaten run in six matches (D-W-W-D-D-D), contrasting Sheffield Wednesday's bottom-table position (24th, already relegated in February with a record-early drop) and winless home form (0-7-15). Both sides grapple with injuries—Owls missing Iké Ugbo, Di'Shon Bernard, Liam Cooper, and others; Baggies without Josh Maja (ankle), Jed Wallace (doubtful leg), and Mikey Johnston (surgery)—yet West Brom's head-to-head edge (8 wins to Wednesday's 4 in recent H2H) and depth position them as clear favorites despite poor away record, with a draw at 20.5% viable in a low-stakes finale for the hosts.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

While odds exceed 90%, scenarios like a high-profile GOP recruit, Houlahan scandal, legal challenges, or a national midterm wave favoring the opposition party could narrow the gap before the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Anysphere (Cursor) IPO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed company completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, as confirmed by official company announcements or credible news sources. The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If the listed company merges with another entity, is acquired, or ceases to exist before the market resolves, the market will also resolve to "No".

This market will resolve early if the listed company completes an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

Discord holds strong around 70% on its January draft registration amid platform growth, while OpenAI's odds slipped to 32% after a Wall Street Journal report highlighted missed revenue targets and escalating data center costs. Upcoming catalysts include S-1 amendments, regulatory approvals, and Q2 earnings, against a backdrop of AI infrastructure competition and favorable market conditions.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Morgan Stanley as the frontrunner at 40.5% implied probability to serve as lead bank for SpaceX's anticipated "Project Apex" IPO, driven by its longstanding ties to Elon Musk—including veteran banker Michael Grimes' February return to the firm—and early reports naming it a top contender among the syndicate. Recent April developments, including SpaceX's virtual banker meeting on April 6 outlining a $75 billion raise at up to $1.75 trillion valuation, prospectus release in late May, and June roadshow, confirmed Morgan Stanley alongside Bank of America (16.6%) and Goldman Sachs (14.5%) as senior bookrunners in a 21-bank group, fueling jockeying for top billing amid retail allocation talks via E*Trade. Lower odds for JPMorgan and others reflect their supporting roles, with resolution hinging on final S-1 designations.

markets.question: Le Mans FC vs. Stade de Reims: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Ligue 2 game between Le Mans FC and Stade de Reims, scheduled for May 2 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Le Mans FC and Stade de Reims combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue2.fr. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Le Mans FC vs. Stade de Reims - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Ligue 2 game, scheduled for May 2 at 2:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Le Mans FC, entrenched in third place on the Ligue 2 table with 15 wins and robust home form (eight victories at Stade Marie-Marvingt), host fourth-placed Stade de Reims on May 2 in a pivotal promotion six-pointer. Le Mans boast momentum from recent triumphs including a 4-0 rout of Pau FC on April 6 and a 4-3 comeback at Amiens, bolstering their clean-sheet streak. Reims remain competitive but stumbled to a 2-2 draw at Laval last week, compounded by off-field disruption from armed fan threats at their training ground five days ago potentially impacting morale. Reims dominate head-to-head (unbeaten in six, including February's 3-0 Coupe de France win), though Le Mans contend without long-term absentee Jean Vercruysse (cruciate ligament tear).

markets.question: Will Ted Cruz post 200+ posts from April 28 to May 5, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Ted Cruz (@tedcruz), posts on X between April 28, 12:00 PM ET and May 5, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Ted Cruz's X posts from April 28 to May 5 reflects his established pattern of 10-15 daily outputs, with 60-79 and 100-119 bins deadlocked at 44.5% amid uncertainty over news-driven spikes. April 28 kicked off strongly with at least eight posts, featuring quote reactions to Ilhan Omar's World War II gaffe, NYC budget woes, EPA rescissions under Lee Zeldin, and SNAP fraud revelations, consistent with his high-engagement style blending policy critiques, memes, and cultural nods like Monty Python. The tight race persists due to variable catalysts—Senate floor fights or viral exchanges historically add 5-10 posts daily—against baselines from prior weeks' markets favoring 80-99 tallies. Absent major hearings or Trump administration announcements, steady commentary on midterms or foreign policy could tip toward higher bins, while quieter schedules favor the low 60s.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects high uncertainty in short-range forecasts for Moscow's unusually cold May 1 high temperature, with market-implied odds tightly clustered at 37% for both 8°C and 9°C amid a broader 35-37% spread across 6-10°C outcomes—well below the historical average of 16°C. This positioning stems from a persistent Arctic air mass over European Russia, reinforced by recent northerly winds and precipitation as shown in the latest ECMWF and GFS model runs over the past 48 hours, which project highs of 7-9°C under overcast skies and light rain. Differentiating factors include ensemble variations in cloud cover timing and frontal passage speed: clearer afternoon scenarios favor 9-10°C, while prolonged overcast conditions tilt toward 6-7°C. New 00Z forecasts from NOAA and ECMWF, due within hours, could sharpen this divergence.

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' overwhelming dominance in the early 2026 Formula 1 season, leading the constructors' standings with 135 points after six Grands Prix, has positioned George Russell and Kimi Antonelli as the clear frontrunners for the drivers' championship, reflecting trader consensus on their superior car package amid new regulations. Antonelli holds a slim nine-point lead at 72 over Russell's 63, boosted by recent victories in China and Shanghai plus strong average finishes around 1.33, while Russell claimed Australia and excels in qualifying head-to-heads. Ferrari's Charles Leclerc trails at 49 points in third, with McLaren's Oscar Piastri and Lando Norris further back due to inconsistent race pace. Pre-Miami GP comments from Russell highlight team weaknesses and intra-team rivalry intensity, yet traders imply a 41.5% probability for Russell's experience to prevail over Antonelli's 31.1% surge.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve has maintained the federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% since January 2026, reflecting resilient economic growth, sticky inflation, and a solid labor market. March CPI surged 3.3% year-over-year—driven by energy prices—while nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs and unemployment held at 4.3%, tempering rate-cut expectations. The March dot plot median projects a modest 3.4% by end-2026, implying one 25-basis-point reduction amid balanced risks. Today's April 28-29 FOMC meeting is poised for no change, with focus shifting to April CPI data on May 12 and the June 16-17 meeting featuring updated projections, as traders weigh inflation trajectory against softening growth signals for the path to 2027.

markets.question: Will New England Patriots win the 2026 AFC East?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the AFC East division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Buffalo Bills hold a slim 53.5% implied probability as AFC East favorites, buoyed by Josh Allen's elite quarterback play and key offseason additions like trading for WR D.J. Moore and signing DE Bradley Chubb, despite a middling D+ draft grade with edge rusher T.J. Parker and CB Davison Igbinosun. New England Patriots surged to 36% after snapping Buffalo's streak with the 2025 division title, bolstered by promising draft picks and free agency depth amid rumors of WR A.J. Brown landing there, narrowing the gap in this closely contested market. Jets (8%) and Dolphins (6.6%) lag amid QB turmoil—New York's $48M dead cap from releasing Aaron Rodgers and Justin Fields, Miami's $52M hit post-Tua Tagovailoa trade to Atlanta.

markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Anyone's Legend and Weibo Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Weibo Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.
markets.groupItemTitle: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)

events.title: LoL: Anyone's Legend vs Weibo Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win against Weibo Gaming in the named LoL match in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win against Anyone's Legend in the LoL match.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anyone's Legend holds the edge in trader consensus for their LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 against Weibo Gaming, riding a hot streak with 2-0 sweeps over Invictus Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas in the past week, propelling them into top-three standings at 5-3 series record. Weibo Gaming secured a morale-boosting 2-0 over Top Esports but sits precariously at 3-6 amid roster upheaval, losing jungler Tian and support Erha in late March. AL boasts a 57% head-to-head win rate, including a 2-0 earlier this split, with Tarzan's jungle pathing and macro sidelane pressure key factors. Top-four spots offer direct playoff berths, heightening stakes under Patch 26.7 meta shifts.

markets.question: Will Australia win the televote for Eurovision 2026?
markets.description:
The Grand Final of the Eurovision Song Contest 2026 is scheduled for May 16, 2026.

This market will resolve to the country that receives the highest number of points from the televote in the Grand Final of the Eurovision Song Contest 2026.

All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

markets.question: Les Wexner charged by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Leslie Wexner between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on an 82% implied probability for no charges against Les Wexner by June 30 reflects the absence of DOJ or FBI criminal action despite intensified scrutiny from early 2026 Epstein file releases. Newly unredacted 2019 documents named Wexner an unindicted co-conspirator in Epstein's sex-trafficking probe, prompting a House Oversight Committee subpoena and February 18 deposition where Wexner testified he was "duped" by Epstein, denied knowledge of crimes, and severed ties in 2007. A civil lawsuit filed April 6 by Epstein survivors alleges Wexner funded Epstein's operations, but no indictments or charges have followed these developments, aligning with historical patterns where Epstein associates faced limited prosecution absent new evidence. Upcoming civil proceedings may add pressure, though criminal thresholds remain unmet.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Moscow be 4°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by NOAA at the Vnukovo International Airport in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=UUWW

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a maximum temperature of 6°C (43.5% implied probability) for Moscow on April 30, driven by the aftermath of a record-shattering April 27 snowstorm that brought over 20 mm of precipitation and plunged daytime highs to +2–4°C amid an Arctic air intrusion. Official forecasts from ensemble models like ECMWF and GFS, alongside sites such as yr.no projecting 3–4°C maxima under persistent cloud cover and light precipitation, reflect subdued warming potential as cold air lingers below the late-April climatological average of 10–12°C. Recent observations through April 29 confirm chilly conditions near 1–5°C, with minor model divergences allowing upside to 7–8°C if high pressure builds; traders await final hourly data from Russian Hydromet stations for resolution.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: EUR/USD trades near 1.1706 amid persistent Federal Reserve-ECB policy divergence, with the US federal funds rate at 3.5-3.75% versus the ECB deposit facility rate at 2%, bolstering dollar strength through higher US yields. Both central banks held rates unchanged at their March 2026 meetings, as Eurozone HICP inflation rose to 2.6%—its highest since mid-2024—while US data shows war-related price pressures delaying Fed cuts into late 2026. Trader consensus prices limited euro appreciation absent convergence, with Eurozone GDP growth forecast at a modest 1.0-1.1%. Key catalysts include today's FOMC statement and the ECB's next policy review, potentially in late April, alongside April CPI releases.

markets.question: Will Microsoft (MSFT) close above $435 end of April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on the final trading day of April 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/MSFT/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former U.S. Rep. Ben McAdams leads trader consensus at 60% implied probability for Utah's 1st Congressional District Democratic primary on June 23, driven by his fundraising dominance—holding as much cash on hand as the rest of the field combined—name recognition from his 2019-2021 service, and a March Data for Progress poll showing 36% support among likely primary voters. Nate Blouin's 26.5% reflects progressive enthusiasm as a state senator criticizing McAdams' moderate record on issues like minimum wage and reproductive rights, amid recent attack ads and debates. At the April 26 Utah Democratic convention, McAdams captured 40% on the first ballot before losing ranked-choice endorsement to newcomer Liban Mohamed, but traders view the delegate-heavy event as left-skewed versus the broader primary electorate, positioning McAdams' base for a plurality win in the newly drawn blue-leaning district. Lower odds for Brian King, Caroline Gleich, and others highlight their limited polling and visibility.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve has maintained the federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% since January 2026, reflecting resilient economic growth, sticky inflation, and a solid labor market. March CPI surged 3.3% year-over-year—driven by energy prices—while nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs and unemployment held at 4.3%, tempering rate-cut expectations. The March dot plot median projects a modest 3.4% by end-2026, implying one 25-basis-point reduction amid balanced risks. Today's April 28-29 FOMC meeting is poised for no change, with focus shifting to April CPI data on May 12 and the June 16-17 meeting featuring updated projections, as traders weigh inflation trajectory against softening growth signals for the path to 2027.

markets.question: Will DeepSeek have the #3 AI model at the end of April 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will the median home value in Miami be less than $1,180,000 on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the median home value for all property types in Miami, Florida on April 30, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket. 

The resolution source will be official data from the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for Miami City. The settlement price will be calculated by multiplying the published price index value (price per square foot) by 2100, which is the median square footage in Miami. Parcl is set to publish this data on April 30, 2026. If no data for April 30 is released by May 10, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve according to the most recently published data. (see: https://app.parcllabs.com/prediction-market-resolutions/23)

Tesla's Q1 earnings on April 24 beat EPS expectations at $0.41 but elicited a muted stock reaction amid softening EV demand and Chinese competition, leaving Musk's net worth around $800-850 billion despite February's SpaceX-xAI merger boosting valuations to $1.25 trillion combined. Absent credible reports of a new child, the parlay's multiplicative improbability dominates sentiment, though Q2 deliveries and potential SpaceX IPO rumors could shift odds.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

events.title: South Korea Annual Inflation 2026
events.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders' skin-in-the-game consensus prices South Korea's 2026 annual CPI inflation above 3.0% at a slim 51% implied probability, edging out the 2.1%-2.3% band at 49.1% following March's YoY rate uptick to 2.2%—driven by oil price surges from U.S.-Iran tensions—though it undershot forecasts at 2.4%. Bank of Korea's April 10 decision to hold the base rate at 2.5% reaffirmed a 2.0% full-year projection amid mid-to-high 2% signals, but OECD's upward revision to 2.7% and AMRO's 2.3% forecast underscore energy-driven upside risks versus potential demand cooling. The tight contest with <1.5% at 49% reflects bets on aggressive monetary tightening; April CPI data and May BoK board meeting loom as pivotal catalysts.

markets.question: Will Caroline Gleich be the Democratic nominee for UT-01?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the UT-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 23, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former U.S. Rep. Ben McAdams leads trader consensus at 60% implied probability for Utah's 1st Congressional District Democratic primary on June 23, driven by his fundraising dominance—holding as much cash on hand as the rest of the field combined—name recognition from his 2019-2021 service, and a March Data for Progress poll showing 36% support among likely primary voters. Nate Blouin's 26.5% reflects progressive enthusiasm as a state senator criticizing McAdams' moderate record on issues like minimum wage and reproductive rights, amid recent attack ads and debates. At the April 26 Utah Democratic convention, McAdams captured 40% on the first ballot before losing ranked-choice endorsement to newcomer Liban Mohamed, but traders view the delegate-heavy event as left-skewed versus the broader primary electorate, positioning McAdams' base for a plurality win in the newly drawn blue-leaning district. Lower odds for Brian King, Caroline Gleich, and others highlight their limited polling and visibility.

markets.question: Will Qinwen Zheng win the 2026 Women’s US Open?
markets.description:
The 2026 U.S. Open tennis tournament is scheduled for August 23 - September 13, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 U.S. Open Women’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 U.S. Open Women’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 U.S. Open Women’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after October 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the U.S. Open (https://www.usopen.org/index.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Aryna Sabalenka commands 35.5% trader consensus for the 2026 US Open women's title, reflecting her World No. 1 dominance with a 25-1 singles record through April, including a fourth-round push in Madrid on clay while extending her hard-court mastery from earlier triumphs like the Australian Open. Elena Rybakina's 18.7% implied probability stems from her Australian Open major victory—her second—and fresh Stuttgart clay title last week, signaling sustained elite form transferable to Flushing Meadows hard courts. Iga Swiatek sits at 16.0% after a third-round retirement in Madrid due to illness, tempering her No. 4 ranking edge despite strong baseline game. Xiyu Wang's elevated 11.7% highlights recent breakthroughs amid clay swings, with the field competitive as summer hard-court prep looms.

markets.question: Will the Bank of Russia decrease the key rate after the July Meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the key rate resulting from the Bank of Russia’s July meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Russia after its July 24, 2026 meeting as listed on the official Bank of Russia calendar: https://www.cbr.ru/eng/dkp/cal_mp/#t13

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Russia’s press release for their July 24, 2026 meeting with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the key rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders' consensus prices a razor-thin edge for no change (52%) over a key rate decrease (48.5%) at the Bank of Russia's July 2026 meeting, reflecting deep uncertainty after the central bank's cautious 50 basis point cut to 14.5% on April 24 amid March inflation holding steady at 5.9% year-over-year, above the 4% target. Persistent price pressures from a tight labor market and fiscal spending clash with early-year GDP contraction of 1.8% in January-February, prompting debate on whether accumulated monetary policy tightness suffices to anchor inflation toward 4% in the second half of 2026 or if further easing is needed. The June meeting and intervening CPI releases will be pivotal swing factors.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) hit (LOW) $264 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Apple (AAPL) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Apple (AAPL) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Apple (AAPL) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Apple (AAPL) shares trade near $270 with a $4 trillion market cap and trailing P/E of 34, as traders position ahead of tomorrow's fiscal Q2 2026 earnings release on April 30, expected to deliver $109-110 billion in revenue and $1.95 EPS amid steady iPhone demand, services expansion, and AI feature rollouts. Analyst consensus points to a $298 average 12-month price target (high $350), reflecting optimism on FY2026 growth to $466 billion revenue, though UBS holds neutral at $287 citing memory cost pressures. Post-earnings guidance will shape May trajectories, with China sales and regulatory scrutiny as key risks; WWDC in June looms as the next catalyst for valuation shifts.
